# Rotorflight Documentation

> Comprehensive documentation for Rotorflight, the open-source helicopter flight controller software.


## rotorflight-docs


### search

- [Search the documentation](/search.md)

### docs

Rotorflight is a flight control software suite for single rotor RC helicopters. It is based on Betaflight, with many advanced features added for helicopters. It consists of several components:

- [Introduction](/docs.md): Rotorflight is a flight control software suite for single rotor RC helicopters. It is based on Betaflight, with many advanced features added for helicopters. It consists of several components:
- [Configurator](/docs/configurator.md)
- [Adjustments](/docs/configurator/tabs/adjustments.md): There are total 32 adjustment slots, each assigning an AUX channel to a configuration parameter or a special function. An adjustment slot consist of an enabling condition, an adjustment function, and parameter limits.
- [Beepers](/docs/configurator/tabs/beepers.md): To Do
- [Blackbox](/docs/configurator/tabs/blackbox.md): What is Blackbox
- [CLI](/docs/configurator/tabs/cli.md): This tab is the CLI or Command Line Interface. It is a method for sending commands and settings to the flight controller. We can also backup and restore our configuration using this tab.
- [Configuration](/docs/configurator/tabs/configuration.md): Personalization
- [Failsafe](/docs/configurator/tabs/failsafe.md): Failsafe Tab
- [GPS](/docs/configurator/tabs/gps.md): Rotorflight currently only uses GPS for telemetry. Return to home, and other autopilot features are not available.
- [Gyro](/docs/configurator/tabs/gyro.md)
- [LED Strip](/docs/configurator/tabs/led-strip.md)
- [Mixer](/docs/configurator/tabs/mixer.md): This section will describe the configuration of the mixer and calibrate the rotor blade angles.
- [Modes](/docs/configurator/tabs/modes.md): Modes
- [Motors](/docs/configurator/tabs/motors.md): The Motors tab is used to configure the ESC communications protocols ( Motor Control Protocol & Telemetry Protocol ) ,as well as to provide the ability to test motor operation. Depending on the selected ESC protocol and selected Rotorflight features, you might need to specify some other parameters as well.
- [Power](/docs/configurator/tabs/power.md): Set up all your power and battery related settings. Set the voltage and current sensor sources and calibration so that the FC can read the values and warn you accordingly. You can also check the current power measurements.
- [Presets](/docs/configurator/tabs/presets.md): Presets Tab
- [Profiles](/docs/configurator/tabs/profiles.md): The purpose of profiles are to store in flight tuning parameters. The aim is usually to have several 'profiles' that you can switch between during flight to change flight performance. Things like different headspeeds, tuning, rescue settings etc can be 'tuned' for the individual flight mode. Please see the profile switching example
- [Rates](/docs/configurator/tabs/rates.md): The purpose of rates are to change in flight sensitivity and rotation rates. The aim is usually to have several 'rates' that you can switch between during flight to change flight performance. Please see the profile switching example
- [Receiver](/docs/configurator/tabs/receiver.md): One of the first things you should do is to connect a receiver to the FC. Your FC has several UARTs, and each UART has a TX and an RX. A receiver needs to be connected to an RX and/or TX pad, depending on the type of receiver.
- [Sensors](/docs/configurator/tabs/sensors.md): On this tab you can enable or disable trending for each sensor. This is for testing and confirmation that each is working correctly.
- [Servos](/docs/configurator/tabs/servos.md): Open the Servos tab and confirm that you have the correct number of servos. If this is not the case, please confirm you have installed the correct firmware (either 'M' motorized or servo tail) or if you are using a drone FC that your Remapping  is correct.
- [Setup](/docs/configurator/tabs/setup.md): Setup
- [Status](/docs/configurator/tabs/status.md): A place for basic flight controller information. The status tab is the first tab you see when you connect to your flight controller. You can check the gyroscope live preview, view the arming prevention flags and other FC information.
- [Adding a Blog Post](/docs/Contributing/Adding-a-Blog.md): To make a new Blog post open the Blog Directory. Click on one of the previous posts to use as a template then chose download.
- [Software Developers](/docs/Contributing/Developers.md): Software developers willing to assist with adding to this project are welcome. Skills in languages such as 'C', HTML, JavaScript, JQuery across the Firmware, Configurator and Blackbox.
- [Editing the Website](/docs/Contributing/Editing-the-Website.md): Website is built using Docusaurus
- [Flow charts/Diagrams](/docs/Contributing/Flow-Charts.md): Using charts, flow diagrams etc
- [Contributing to Rotorflight](/docs/Contributing/intro.md): Rotorflight is an Open Source project. Contribution from other like minded people, keen to assist in making this software even better is welcomed and encouraged. You do not have to be a hardcore programmer to help. Lots of things can be done by anyone with a spare heli and some eagerness to learn and teach others. If you have the skills and time to contribute to some of the activities below please contact us through the Discord and we can chat about the options.
- [Modeling Cross Coupling](/docs/Contributing/Modeling-Cross-Coupling.md): By Etocii
- [Support Us!](/docs/Contributing/support.md): We hope you enjoy using Rotorflight. If so please consider supporting us with a donation to our project. You can do a one time donation or a recurring one.
- [Controllers](/docs/controllers.md): There are several flight controllers specifically made for Rotorflight.
- [Diy board - Betaflight FC](/docs/controllers/betaflight-diy.md): Rotorflight is a fork of the popular Betaflight project commonly used for drones. This means that all of the drone boards designed for betaflight are also compatible with Rotorflight. Drones most commonly use 4 motors and do not have servos so we need to do some additional configuration to add these items (as shown below).
- [Flydragon V2.2](/docs/controllers/flydragon2.2.md): Flydragon v2.2
- [Flywing Heli405](/docs/controllers/flywingf405heli.md): Flywing Heli405
- [FrSky Vantac RF007](/docs/controllers/frsky-007.md): FrSky Vantac RF007
- [Matek G474Heli](/docs/controllers/matek_heli.md): Matek G474HELI
- [Matek G474-HLite](/docs/controllers/matek_hlite.md): Matek G474HELI
- [Radiomaster NEXUS](/docs/controllers/rm-nexus.md): Nexus
- [Download - Blackbox](/docs/download/blackbox.md): Download the Release v2.2.0
- [Download - Configurator](/docs/download/configurator.md): Rotorflight Release v2.2.1
- [Download - Edge Tx Lua](/docs/download/edge-tx-Lua.md): Download the EdgeTx Release v2.2.1
- [Download - Ethos Tx Lua](/docs/download/ethos-Lua.md): Versions
- [v2.2.1 Upgrade notes](/docs/download/notes.md): Update procedure
- [Getting Started](/docs/examples.md): The general process for setting up a helicopter using Rotorflight.
- [Example setup - Servo tail](/docs/examples/example-1.md): The following doc will give and overview for how to setup a servo tailed helicopter using Rotorflight. The purpose is just as a generic guide to outline the steps to take when setting up a helicopter of this type.
- [Design Requirements](/docs/Manufacturers/Design-Requirements.md): This page collects the generic hardware requirements for all Flight Controller designs for Rotorflight.
- [Manufacturers Information](/docs/Manufacturers/intro.md): The Rotorflight team have collaborated with several manufacturers to produce Flight Controllers for use with our software. We welcome working with other vendors and designers. Please contact us on admin@rotorflight.org or on the Discord and we can discuss further.
- [Reference Design](/docs/Manufacturers/Reference-Design.md): Design
- [Arming](/docs/setup/arming.md): Arming is a safety feature that checks a series of settings in the FC to make sure it is safe before allowing it to spool up. It checks for things like accelerometer calibration, gyro filtering, throttle not at minimum, RX not connected.
- [Backup and restore](/docs/setup/backup-and-restore.md): Back up the configuration
- [Flashing Blheli_S ESC to Bluejay firmware](/docs/setup/blheli_s-to-bluejay.md): Rotorflight requires motor RPM telemetry to provide the Governor function and filtering of the gyro signal. This can be provided by the ESC via bidirectional D-shot telemetry. Blheli\_S ESC's do not provide this feature as standard and the firmware has to be updated. Bluejay has been tested with many Rotorflight builds and is preferred due to the ability to disable Damped Light mode and loading individual ESC config.
- [ELRS Custom Telemetry](/docs/setup/elrs-custom-telemetry.md): There is a bug in ELRS prior to 3.5.5 that can lead to loss of control.\
- [ESC Forward Programming](/docs/setup/esc-forward-programming.md): Forward programming enables the connected ESC to be programmed via your transmitter rather than requiring a vendor programming box.
- [ESC Telemetry](/docs/setup/esc-telemetry.md): The purpose of ESC telemetry is to provide a method of reading the values provided by the ESC. These are things such as RPM, Temperature, Voltage, Current and mAh consumed. This is accomplished by connecting the ESC telemetry wire to the flight controller with a spare UART.
- [Adding an extra servo](/docs/setup/extra-servo.md): Add an extra servo controlled by an AUX channel.
- [Flashing the firmware](/docs/setup/flashing-the-firmware.md): Install Rotorflight Configurator
- [Governor](/docs/setup/governor.md): The purpose of the governor is to maintain a constant headspeed regardless of flight inputs, flying conditions, battery voltage, external disturbances, etc. In Rotorflight, the governor also provides other motor related features, like slow spoolup, autorotation control, battery voltage drop compensation, and fault detection and recovery.
- [Flyrotor Governor setup](/docs/setup/governor-flyrotor-setup.md): ESC Configurator
- [Governor setup](/docs/setup/governor-setup.md): There are several options for configuring the Governor.
- [Getting started with LEDs](/docs/setup/led-strip-quick-start.md): Introduction to LED strips
- [Lua Scripts](/docs/setup/lua-scripts.md): Introduction
- [OpenLager](/docs/setup/openlager.md): OpenLager is a Blackbox data logger developed by dRonin.
- [Profile (Bank) switching example](/docs/setup/profile-switching-example.md): Purpose
- [EdgeTX Generic Radio Setup](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-edgetx/edgetx-generic-radio-setup.md): This walkthrough will guide you through to create a basic radio model for rotorflight controller.
- [RF2 Touch Lua Scripts](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-edgetx/lua-RF2-touch.md): By Shmuley
- [ETX Widget](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-edgetx/widget-etx-bob00.md): Bob00 has created a custom widget for displaying Rotorflight Telemetry. More details and downloads can be found in the Github Repository.
- [FrSky Ethos Background Task Notification](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-ethos/ethos-background-task-notification.md): On a new installation of the Ethos Suite you may be presented with the error enable background task.
- [ETHOS Generic Radio Setup](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-ethos/ethos-generic-radio-setup.md): This walkthrough will guide you through to create a basic radio model for rotorflight controller.
- [FrSky Ethos RFStatus LUA Script](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-ethos/ethos-nexus-status.md): Installation
- [FrSky Ethos example](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-ethos/ethos-setup.md): Instructions for Frsky Ethos Initial Radio and Configurator Setup, this is a generic setup using an Frsky transmitter with an frsky access receiver with a Rotorflight FC. This example shows a Nexus; however, the process is the same for any Rotorflight controller. Please choose the port related to your FC.
- [FrSky Ethos Ethos simulators](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-ethos/ethos-simulators.md): The following links will take you to the rotorflight Ethos suite simulators.
- [FrSky Ethos Missing Sensors](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-ethos/ethos-suite-missing-sensors.md): This guide will help with the error shown below: This error is shown where the Ethos Suite LUA is not receiving a sensor(s) from the FBL it requires to operate.
- [Futaba example](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-futaba/futaba-setup.md): Instructions for Futaba Initial Radio and Configurator Setup, this is a generic setup using Futaba T26SZ transmitter and SBUS2 receiver with a Rotorflight FC. This example shows a Nexus; however, the process is the same for any Rotorflight controller. Please choose the SBUS port related to your FC
- [Jeti example](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-jeti/jeti-setup.md): Instructions for Jeti Initial Radio and Configurator Setup, this is a generic setup using a Frsky transmitter with a jeti exbus receiver with a Rotorflight FC. This example shows a Nexus; however, the process is the same for any Rotorflight controller. Please choose the SBUS port related to your FC.
- [Other Transmitters](/docs/setup/radio-setup/radio-setup-others/others.md): Coming Soon
- [Remapping (for DIY boards)](/docs/setup/remapping.md): Rotorflight is based on Betaflight which is generally used to control drones. Unfortunately, drones do not have servos and have more motors than we need for a helicopter. In order to use servos with a Betaflight controller we must remap the pins so we have a pad to connect the servos to.
- [RPM Filters](/docs/setup/rpm-filters.md): Introduction
- [RPM Measurement](/docs/setup/rpm-measurement.md): In order to use the Governor or RPM filters (and why wouldn't you!!) you must measure the RPM. There are two options for this:
- [Mixer Setup and Calibration](/docs/setup/setup-mixer.md): Before starting on this step the Servos should already be connected and calibrated.
- [Nitro/gasser throttle servo setup](/docs/setup/setup-nitro.md): 1. First plug in the throttle servo into the ESC port. Go to the motors page.
- [Servo Setup and Calibration](/docs/setup/setup-servos.md): Step 1 - set frequency and center
- [Swashplate Wiggle](/docs/setup/swash-wiggle.md): The swashplate can be configured to 'wiggle' as an indication for when you are
- [Two-Stage Gear Train Ratios](/docs/setup/two-stage-gear-train-ratios.md): Having an accurate gear ratio for the main and tail rotor is essential for Rotorflight accurate RPM Filters performance.
- [Using Stability Modes (6G)](/docs/setup/using-stability-modes-example.md): Purpose
- [Tuning process](/docs/testing/tuning-process.md): Collective to Pitch Compensation
- [Cyclic Cross Coupling](/docs/Tuning/Cyclic-Cross-Coupling.md): What is it?
- [First Flight & Filter Tuning](/docs/Tuning/First-Flight-Filter-Tuning.md): It is crucial to tune the filters based on your helicopter to gain the maximum performance while keeping noise out of the control loop. A good filter tuning should be just strong enough to suppress the peaks in the gyro readouts. A filter too weak may have peaks leaked inside the loop, and a filter too strong, while making the gyro signal clean, may lower the maximum gains later which cause a "sloppy" and delayed feel.
- [High Speed Integral](/docs/Tuning/High-Speed-Integral.md): TL;DR
- [Motorised Tail and TTA](/docs/Tuning/Motorised-Tail-and-TTA.md): Torque Tail Assist (TTA) - This is a feature used for helicopters with motorised tails. A motorised tail can provide high control authority in one direction. In the other it is greatly reduced as the motor can only turn in one direction. TTA is used to add control authority in the other direction. It does this by rapidly accelerating the main motor. This acceleration provides torque in the opposite direction to the tail motor.
- [Rescue mode settings](/docs/Tuning/Rescue-mode-settings.md)
- [Tune Feedforward](/docs/Tuning/Tune-Feedforward.md): It is a good practice to check for the feed forward tune after adjusting other parameters. To do this, you would need to have some basic 3D skills to do continuous flips and rolls.
- [Tune the Governor](/docs/Tuning/Tune-Governor.md): If using the governor you must first enable the RPM Measurement and configure the Governor
- [Tuning your Helicopter](/docs/Tuning/Tuning-description.md): The default tuning parameters supplied with Rotorflight are designed to be safe and flyable for all helicopters. This means in most cases the helicopter will not be responsive and locked in on your first flight and some tuning will be required.
- [Tuning examples and presets](/docs/Tuning/tuning-examples.md): Please use the supplied defaults until a maiden flight has been conducted and the filtering is confirmed. Once this is done higher gains can be tested. Skipping this step and using these settings without adequate filtering could result in an uncontrollable helicopter.
